critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — SAFETY-NET DEPLOYMENT WITHOUT VALIDATION: Model not validated in the safety-net setting. Extension in 6 months creates an unquantified distribution shift risk.

C2 — THRESHOLD WITHOUT COST-BENEFIT: 80th percentile threshold chosen on capacity grounds. Break-even analysis at $45/$12,000 ratio is missing.

C3 — HRRP NOT ANALYZED: HRRP penalty exposure depends on performance across specific diagnosis groups. Not evaluated.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — Concede the validation gap for the safety-net extension. Six months provides time for prospective validation if planned now. This should be a gate condition for the extension.

C2 — Concede. Break-even analysis is feasible pre-launch and should be completed.

C3 — Concede. HRRP stratified analysis can be run on the 12-month holdout before launch.

All three concerns are pre-deployment analysis requirements, not model rejections.
adjudication_raw: Full concession by defender on all three points. Empirical test conditions specified. Deployment to existing hospitals may proceed with threshold analysis; safety-net extension gated on prospective validation; HRRP analysis before launch.

empirical_test.measure: Prospective safety-net hospital validation AUC-ROC; threshold precision vs. cost-ratio break-even; HRRP-stratified holdout recall
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC not materially degraded; threshold precision above break-even; HRRP diagnosis groups adequately recalled
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net performance substantially degraded; threshold precision below break-even; HRRP groups systematically under-identified
